summary: Backend-only run for the new Owner Admin Secrets endpoints + regression. 22/22 PASS in the new admin suite and 56/56 PASS across the full backend test directory (~86s). Verified: GET /api/admin/me returns is_admin:true for demo@example.com and false for alice@example.com; GET /api/admin/stripe-key returns mode + fingerprint and is admin-gated; POST /api/admin/stripe-key rejects empty key, rejects non-sk_test_/sk_live_ prefixes (400), rejects invalid Stripe keys with the upstream Stripe error wrapped in 400, accepts the special `sk_test_emergent` placeholder WITHOUT hitting Stripe and hot-swaps in-process; DELETE /api/admin/stripe-key reverts to sandbox, persists to .env, and the GET reflects it. Auth gate: all /api/admin/* without bearer → 401, non-admin (alice) → 403. .env atomic rewrite verified — exactly one STRIPE_API_KEY=, STRIPE_KEY_FINGERPRINT=, STRIPE_KEY_UPDATED_AT= line each (no append duplicates). Regression: /auth/login, /auth/me, /checkout/create (sandbox), /referrals/me, /creations, /creations/stats all green. .env restored to STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent at end of run as requested.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1040 lines — past the 700-line threshold. Recommend splitting into modules (auth.py, admin.py, checkout.py, generation.py, referrals.py) before further feature growth.

_persist_env_var() writes a temp file then replaces it — good. But it also rewrites lines that may contain expanded $vars or quotes if the user ever puts them in .env. Currently safe because no other key uses $, but worth a note.

ensure_admin() has a 'first user becomes admin' fallback. Combined with ADMIN_EMAIL=demo@example.com this is fine for this app, but in a multi-tenant deployment that fallback could surprise operators. Consider gating it behind a feature flag (e.g. ALLOW_FIRST_USER_ADMIN=true).

POST /api/admin/stripe-key allows ANY caller-supplied sk_live_ key once it passes Stripe /v1/balance. There is no audit log of who rotated the key beyond a single logger.info line. For Google Play production, persist rotations to a `admin_audit` collection (who, when, fingerprint, mode).
_validate_stripe_key uses a 10s timeout against api.stripe.com — fine, but on transient Stripe outage admin rotation will 400. Acceptable for now; document it in the admin UI.

success_rate.backend: 100% (56/56 across full suite; 22/22 new admin suite)

seed_data_creation: Reused existing demo@example.com and alice@example.com (auto-registered alice if absent). No new persistent admin/Stripe data; .env restored to sk_test_emergent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. The admin suite is self-contained and idempotent: it always leaves STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent in /app/backend/.env via a module-level autouse teardown fixture that calls DELETE /api/admin/stripe-key. If you add LIVE-mode tests later, do NOT commit a real sk_live_ key — use a dedicated restricted-mode key. The 'rejects invalid stripe key' test relies on Stripe returning 401 for an obviously fake key; if Stripe ever rate-limits the preview egress IP, that single test could flake — retry once.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no failures. New admin/Stripe-rotation surface is fully green and prior endpoints unaffected.
